title: Adana Kebab

seo.title: Adana Kebab Recipe: Authentic Turkish Kebab at Home
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Adana Kebab at home with our easy-to-follow Turkish recipe. Juicy, spicy lamb skewers perfect for lunch or dinner. Get the best Adana Kebab recipe here!
seo.h1: Adana Kebab Recipe: Make Authentic Turkish Kebab at Home
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Adana Kebab
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Adana Kebab
Pro Tips for the Best Adana Kebab Results
Frequently Asked Questions About Adana Kebab
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Adana Kebab skewers served with fresh vegetables and flatbread
seo.imageCaption: Savor the authentic taste of Turkey with our homemade Adana Kebab recipe, perfect for a flavorful lunch.

description: Adana Kebab is a classic Turkish dish featuring hand-minced lamb, seasoned with red pepper paste and spices, then grilled on wide skewers. It's renowned for its rich, spicy flavor and juicy texture, making it a beloved staple in Turkish cuisine. This robust and satisfying meal is perfect for a hearty lunch or a delightful dinner.

ingredients.notes: finely grated and squeezed of excess liquid

instructions.title: Prepare the Vegetables
instructions.text: Finely chop the red and green bell peppers. Grate the onion and squeeze out any excess liquid using a cheesecloth or paper towel. Mince the garlic cloves. It is crucial for vegetables to be very finely prepared to integrate well with the meat.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the finely minced ground lamb, prepared red and green bell peppers, squeezed onion, minced garlic, red pepper paste, pul biber, ground cumin, salt, and black pepper. Ensure all ingredients are evenly distributed.

instructions.title: Knead the Kebab Mixture
instructions.text: Using your hands, thoroughly knead the mixture for at least 10-15 minutes. This process is vital for developing the texture of the kebab, making it sticky and ensuring it adheres to the skewers without falling off during cooking. The mixture should become smooth and pliable.

instructions.title: Form the Kebabs
instructions.text: Divide the kneaded meat mixture into 8-10 equal portions. Take a portion and firmly press it around wide, flat metal skewers, shaping it into a long, uniform kebab, about 1-inch thick and 8-10 inches long. Ensure the meat is tightly packed around the skewer. Repeat with the remaining mixture.

instructions.title: Cook the Adana Kebabs
instructions.text: Preheat your grill to medium-high heat. Alternatively, you can use a grill pan or oven broiler. Place the skewers on the hot grill and cook for about 4-6 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until the kebabs are nicely charred and cooked through. The internal temperature should reach 160°F (71°C).

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Carefully remove the cooked Adana Kebabs from the grill. Serve hot, traditionally with warm flatbread, fresh chopped parsley, a simple sumac onion salad, and sliced tomatoes or roasted peppers. Enjoy the authentic flavors of Turkey!

tips: For authentic Adana Kebab texture, use lamb with at least 20-25% fat and ensure it is finely minced. Kneading the mixture thoroughly for 10-15 minutes is crucial for the meat to bind properly and stick to the skewers.

If you cannot find pul biber (Aleppo pepper), you can substitute it with a mix of sweet paprika and a pinch of cayenne pepper, adjusting to your desired level of spice. Using genuine Turkish red pepper paste (biber salçası) significantly enhances the flavor.

Adana Kebab is best served immediately off the grill. To store leftovers, remove meat from skewers, cool completely, and refrigerate in an airtight container for up to 3 days. Reheat gently in a pan or oven.
article: Adana Kebab is a beloved Turkish Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
